excerpt: Ramaḍān is one of the names of Allah and is the best of all the months. In this month, angels can be seen and the gates of heaven are opened for good deeds.
The inner meaning of fasting is to stop the boundaries of religion from explaining and explaining the inner meaning of the Book of Allah and the Shariah to the people.
source_location_hint: On taʾwīl and taqiyyah in ritual practice

excerpt: In the same way, the obligation of fasting was enforced for the protection of the religion so that the believers could be protected from the evil of the wicked and the security and survival of the religion would be maintained.
source_location_hint: On the protective function of taqiyyah and ritual concealment

excerpt: All these limits are 30 in total, which is compared to the 30 days of Ramadan. There are six speech-levels, seven imams, twelve limits, and five upper limits.
source_location_hint: On the sacred numerology of thirty and the hierarchy of limits

excerpt: Just as the sun illuminates all the universes and other objects, and illuminates them with its light, so the Prophet gives the light of the knowledge of monotheism to all the souls of the world through his divine knowledge.
source_work: Kitab al-ʿAlim wa-l-Ghulam or Asrar al-Nutaqaʾ
source_location_hint: On the prophetic function as universal illumination

excerpt: The Messenger of Allah opened a thousand gates of knowledge to me by his wisdom, and from each door a thousand more doors were opened to me.
source_location_hint: Sermon on the transmission of prophetic knowledge

excerpt: I am the city of knowledge and ʿAlī is its gate, and whoever seeks knowledge should enter through the gate. From today onwards, ʿAlī is their master (Ghadir Khumm declaration).
source_authority: Ismaili tradition of the Prophet's successor
source_location_hint: On the functions of the waṣī as guardian of knowledge
